beginner.question: What does align-content do in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: align-content distributes space between and around wrapped flex lines along the cross axis.

beginner.question: When does align-content have an effect?
beginner.answer: align-content only works when flex-wrap is set to wrap or wrap-reverse and there are multiple lines of items.

beginner.question: What are the values of align-content?
beginner.answer: The values are stretch, flex-start, flex-end, center, space-between, space-around, space-evenly.

beginner.question: What is the default value of align-content?
What does align-content: stretch do?
beginner.answer: stretch distributes wrapped lines evenly to fill the entire cross-axis space of the container.

beginner.question: What does align-content: flex-start do?
beginner.answer: flex-start packs all wrapped lines at the start of the cross axis.

beginner.question: What does align-content: center do?
beginner.answer: center packs wrapped lines in the center of the cross axis.

beginner.question: What does align-content: space-between do?
beginner.answer: space-between distributes wrapped lines evenly, with the first line at the start and the last at the end.

beginner.question: What does align-content: space-around do?
beginner.answer: space-around distributes lines with equal space around each line, but edge spaces are half the interior spaces.

beginner.question: What is the difference between align-items and align-content?
beginner.answer: align-items aligns individual items within a line; align-content distributes multiple lines within the container.

intermediate.question: Why does align-content not work with flex-wrap: nowrap?
intermediate.answer: With nowrap, there is only one flex line, so there are no wrapped lines to distribute; align-content is ignored.

intermediate.question: How does align-content differ from justify-content?
intermediate.answer: justify-content distributes items along the main axis; align-content distributes wrapped lines along the cross axis.

intermediate.question: How do you vertically center wrapped rows using align-content?
intermediate.answer: Use align-content: center on a multi-line flex container with flex-wrap: wrap.

intermediate.question: How does align-content: space-evenly differ from space-around?
intermediate.answer: space-evenly gives equal spacing at edges and between lines; space-around gives half spacing at the edges.

intermediate.question: How does align-content work with flex-direction: column and wrap?
intermediate.answer: In column direction, align-content distributes wrapped columns along the horizontal cross axis.

intermediate.question: What is required for align-content to show visible results?
intermediate.answer: The container must have extra space on the cross axis beyond what the wrapped lines occupy.

intermediate.question: How does align-content: stretch distribute extra space?
intermediate.answer: stretch evenly expands each flex line to fill the cross-axis space, increasing the cross-axis size of each line.

intermediate.question: How does align-content interact with the gap property?
intermediate.answer: gap adds fixed spacing between lines in addition to the distribution provided by align-content.

intermediate.question: How does align-content handle single-line layouts?
intermediate.answer: With a single line (or nowrap), align-content has no effect because there are no multiple lines to distribute.

intermediate.question: How do align-content and align-items work together?
intermediate.answer: align-content distributes the lines, then align-items positions items within each line's cross-axis space.

advanced.question: How does align-content: stretch differ from stretch as an align-items value?
advanced.answer: align-content stretch expands entire flex lines to fill the container; align-items stretch expands individual items to fill a line.

advanced.question: How does align-content behave when the container is taller than the total line height?
advanced.answer: The extra vertical space is distributed according to the align-content value (e.g., center centers all lines).

advanced.question: How does align-content interact with min-height on the flex container?
advanced.answer: min-height sets a floor for the container; align-content distributes space only if the container is larger than the content.

advanced.question: How does the safe modifier affect align-content distribution?
advanced.answer: safe prevents data loss by falling back to a safer alignment if the items overflow the container.

advanced.question: How does align-content work in a flex container with variable line heights?
advanced.answer: Each line retains its height based on content; align-content distributes the remaining space between and around these variable-height lines.

advanced.question: How does align-content differ between Flexbox and CSS Grid?
advanced.answer: In Flexbox, align-content distributes flex lines; in Grid, it distributes grid tracks along the block axis.

advanced.question: How does align-content: space-between behave with uneven line counts?
advanced.answer: Space is distributed evenly between lines; uneven line sizes still get equal spacing between them.

advanced.question: How does align-content affect the scrollable overflow of a flex container?
advanced.answer: If content overflows, align-content may clip items at the edges; overflow: auto can reveal clipped content.

advanced.question: How does align-content work with the place-content shorthand?
advanced.answer: place-content: center center sets both align-content and justify-content to center.

advanced.question: How does the CSS Box Alignment spec define align-content's behavior for replaced elements as lines?
advanced.answer: Each flex line is treated as a distribution unit; replaced elements within lines do not affect the algorithm.
